city_report.transport: Land transport: Taxis to the old town should cost €7–10. The Bolt app is more popular than Uber (it's Estonian after all!) #2 is diverted until 2025 and doesn't stop at the airport – it will resume when construction of the Rail Baltica terminal has reached a point where the tram can pass under the railway line.

attraction.name: Old Town
attraction.description: medieval heart of Tallinn, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

attraction.name: Toompea Hill
attraction.description: Historic hill with the castle and parliament.

attraction.name: Nevsky Cathedral
attraction.description: Grand Orthodox cathedral with onion domes.

attraction.name: Kadriorg Park
attraction.description: Park and palace built by Peter the Great.

attraction.name: Seaplane Harbour
attraction.description: Maritime museum in historic seaplane hangars.

attraction.name: Town Hall Square
attraction.description: The social heart of the city since the 13th century.

attraction.name: Telliskivi
attraction.description: Creative city hub with restaurants and galleries.

attraction.name: Kumu Art Museum
attraction.description: Main museum for Estonian and international art.

attraction.name: St. Olaf's Church
attraction.description: Gothic church once the tallest building in the world.

attraction.name: Fat Margaret
attraction.description: Historic gate tower and maritime museum.

city_report.description: La capitale digitale d'Europa, dove ogni servizio è a portata di click e la sicurezza è garantita da tecnologie avanzate. Il suo centro medievale è perfettamente preservato in un ecosistema high-tech.
city_report.wiki_intro: Tallinn is Estonia's capital and largest city. Tallinn is an important port of the Baltic Sea, with the busy passenger section of the port reaching the foothill of the picturesque medieval Old Town, which has been astonishingly well preserved and is a since 1997. On the shore of the Gulf of Finland, it is a city of over 450,000 inhabitants (2023). It is home to a third of the country's...
